summary:
## Purpose
Handles durable writes of observation records to the observations database. Resolves pronominal ambiguity in LLM-generated summaries at write time by injecting recent prior observations as context. Must tolerate brief service-restart gaps triggered by the health coordinator's auto-heal kickstart mechanism.

## Architecture
- **Prior-context window injection**: before invoking the LLM summarizer, <USER_ID_REDACTED> the observations DB for the 2 most recent observations for the same project + agent within the last 30 minutes. Injects them as `<prior_observations>` XML in the summarization prompt. Only fires when a qualifying prior exists; first-in-session writes receive no injection.
- **Retry loop**: configurable budget (number of retries × delay) wraps the DB write. Budget must span the full kickstart window; an undersized budget silently drops records during mode transitions or escalation sequences.
- **Semantic dedup**: duplicate observations are silently dropped at write time; the drop count is reported in run output but rows are not stored.
- **`init()` requirement**: `ObservationWriter.init()` must be called before the write loop in any consuming script (including backfill scripts); skipping it causes dedup and initialization failures.
- **Phase 50 complement (planned)**: an async post-write LSL resolver will handle residual cases where no prior observation exists at write time.

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationWriter.js` — retry loop, budget config, prior-context window query, `<prior_observations>` prompt injection, semantic dedup

## Usage
Called by the coordinator and related logging infrastructure whenever an observation event is emitted. Also consumed by backfill scripts, which must call `init()` before invoking the write loop. Critical during network-mode transitions (auto-heal kickstarts).

## Troubleshooting
- **Pronoun antecedent loss in tier-1 summaries**: prior-context window covers same project + agent within 30 min; first-in-session or stale priors receive no injection — Phase 50 async resolver will address these via verbatim transcripts
- **Observation writes failing around network mode transitions**: retry budget (retries × interval) must exceed the kickstart window duration including escalated recovery sequences
- **Missing rows after a mode flip or escalation step**: correlate observation gaps with kickstart timestamps in coordinator logs to confirm budget exhaustion before service recovery
- **Unexpected duplicate rows not being deduped**: verify `ObservationWriter.init()` was called before the write loop

topic: Private History Repo — Bootstrap and Migration
summary:
## Purpose
Prevents Claude session transcripts (chat logs) from leaking into public GitHub repositories by storing `.specstory/history/` in a dedicated private `<project>-history` repo on GHE for every project managed by `coding/bin/coding`.

## Architecture
- **Bootstrap function**: `ensure_private_history_repo` in `scripts/agent-common-setup.sh`
  - Derives default history remote URL by appending `-history` to the outer repo's remote name
  - Prompts interactively on first run with skip/accept/override options; writes a skip marker if declined
  - Silently skips repos where `.specstory/history/.git` already exists
  - Supports `LSL_HISTORY_AUTO` and `LSL_HISTORY_REMOTE_TEMPLATE` env vars for CI/non-interactive contexts
  - Falls back to `gh`-based hints on push failure
- **Migration script**: `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh`
  - Uses `git-filter-repo` to purge `.specstory/history/` from public repo history
  - Includes preflight checks (tooling availability, clean working tree)
  - Pushes purged history to corresponding GHE private repo (`<ORG>.ghe.com/<USER>/<REPO>-history`)
- **Canonical private remote**: `Frank-Woernle/coding-history` on GHE for the `coding` project; confirmed as sole remote origin for `.specstory/history/`

## Key Files
- `scripts/agent-common-setup.sh` — contains `ensure_private_history_repo` function
- `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh` — bulk migration script for public repos with leaked history

## Usage
`ensure_private_history_repo` runs automatically when `coding/bin/coding` initialises a project. For existing public repos with leaked history, run `migrate-history-to-private.sh` per-repo after confirming `git-filter-repo` is available.

## Troubleshooting
- **Bootstrap silently skipped**: a skip marker exists from a prior declined prompt — remove the marker file to re-trigger the prompt
- **Malformed GHE remote URL**: URL parser bug producing `ghe.com///...` — verify the HTTPS-URL parsing logic in `migrate-history-to-private.sh`
- **Corporate remote not force-pushed after purge**: some repos have secondary remotes (e.g., `cc-github.bmwgroup.net`) that must be force-pushed separately — `migrate-history-to-private.sh` only handles the primary remote
- **`git-filter-repo` not found**: install via `pip install git-filter-repo`; the script's preflight check will block execution if missing

  - Silently skips repos where `.specstory/history/.git` already exists
  - Supports `LSL_HISTORY_AUTO` and `LSL_HISTORY_REMOTE_TEMPLATE` env vars for CI/non-interactive contexts
  - Falls back to `gh`-based hints on push failure
- **Migration script**: `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh`
  - Uses `git-filter-repo` to purge `.specstory/history/` from public repo history
  - Includes preflight checks (tooling availability, clean working tree)
  - Pushes purged history to corresponding GHE private repo (`<ORG>.ghe.com/<USER>/<REPO>-history`)
- **Canonical private remote**: `Frank-Woernle/coding-history` on GHE for the `coding` project; confirmed as sole remote origin for `.specstory/history/`

## Key Files
- `scripts/agent-common-setup.sh` — contains `ensure_private_history_repo` function
- `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh` — bulk migration script for public repos with leaked history

## Usage
`ensure_private_history_repo` runs automatically when `coding/bin/coding` initialises a project. For existing public repos with leaked history, run `migrate-history-to-private.sh` per-repo after confirming `git-filter-repo` is available.

## Troubleshooting
- **Bootstrap silently skipped**: a skip marker exists from a prior declined prompt — remove the marker file to re-trigger the prompt
- **Malformed GHE remote URL**: URL parser bug producing `ghe.com///...` — verify the HTTPS-URL parsing logic in `migrate-history-to-private.sh`
- **Corporate remote not force-pushed after purge**: some repos have secondary remotes (e.g., `cc-github.bmwgroup.net`) that must be force-pushed separately — `migrate-history-to-private.sh` only handles the primary remote
- **`git-filter-repo` not found**: install via `pip install git-filter-repo`; the script's preflight check will block execution if missing
metadata.scope: `scripts/agent-common-setup.sh` and `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh` — GHE private history repo lifecycle

topic: GitHub Submodule Push — SSH vs HTTPS Authentication
summary:
## Purpose
Documents authentication patterns, failure modes, and resolution procedures for git operations across the multi-submodule outer `coding` repository, where SSH key identity, HTTPS fallbacks, and Git version behavior changes all interact.

## Architecture
- **Submodule inventory**: `lib/km-core`, `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis`, `code-graph-rah`, `constraint-monitor`, `mcp-constraint-monitor`, and the main repo
- **SSH key identity (resolved)**: `ssh-agent` may present a work/corporate key before the personal key, causing GitHub to authenticate as the wrong account. This is resolved by `IdentitiesOnly yes` in the `Host github.com` block of `~/.ssh/config`, which forces SSH to use only `id_ed25519_personal`. Root cause is always in `~/.ssh/config`, not in any agent or IDE config
- **HTTPS fallback**: Switching a remote from SSH to HTTPS (`git remote set-url origin https://github.com/...`) bypasses SSH key selection entirely and is useful for one-off operations; `ssh -T <EMAIL_REDACTED>` confirms which identity SSH is currently resolving before committing to a switch
- **Git ≥2.27 pull behavior**: `git pull` requires an explicit reconciliation strategy; if local commits are a strict subset of upstream, use `git reset --hard origin/main`; if local has unique commits, use `git pull --rebase origin main`
- **Submodule pointer management**: pointer updates in submodule directories require a separate `git commit` in the outer repo to persist

## Key Files
- `~/.ssh/config` — `Host github.com` block; `IdentitiesOnly yes` + `IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_personal` prevent wrong-account authentication (see *Git SSH Connectivity Through HTTP Proxy* insight for full block specification)
- `.gitmodules` — submodule remote URL declarations; SSH vs HTTPS choice made here per-submodule
- `.git/modules/` — per-submodule git state; lock files appear here during index conflicts

## Usage
Applies to all `git push`, `git pull`, and submodule bump workflows in the `coding` project. After bumping a submodule pointer, always verify with `git status` that the submodule directory appears as a modified file in the outer repo, then stage and commit the pointer change separately.

## Troubleshooting
- **SSH push authenticates as QCOM/corporate account**: `ssh-agent` is presenting a work key first — primary fix is `IdentitiesOnly yes` in `~/.ssh/config` `Host github.com` block (see *Git SSH Connectivity Through HTTP Proxy*); secondary option is switching the remote to HTTPS or adding a `Host github.com-personal` alias
- **`git pull --tags origin main` fails with "need to specify how to reconcile" (Git ≥2.27)**: If local is a strict subset of upstream, run `git reset --hard origin/main`; if local has unique commits, run `git pull --rebase origin main`
- **`.git/index.lock` error during submodule operations**: A concurrent git process created the lock — wait for it to clear or remove `.git/index.lock` manually after confirming no other git operation is running
- **Submodule pointer not persisted after bump**: Verify with `git status` that the submodule directory appears as modified in the outer repo, then stage and commit it separately
- **HTTPS push prompts for credentials repeatedly**: Personal access token not stored — add to macOS keychain via `git credential approve` or write to `~/.netrc`
- **Outer repo ahead of `origin/main` after submodule bumps**: Normal after bumping multiple submodules; push with `git push origin main` or via PR if direct push is restricted

topic: System Health Dashboard — Consolidation Progress UI
summary:
## Purpose
Provides developers with live visibility into consolidation run progress, elapsed time, and stuck-process detection within the system health dashboard.

## Architecture
- **Shared component**: `src/components/consolidation-progress.tsx` renders a progress bar, elapsed time, last activity timestamp, and current step label — used by both the digests and insights dashboard pages
- **Heartbeat polling**: the frontend polls independently of the POST response, so the button clears even if the POST response is lost
- **`stderrAgeMs`**: `server.js` computes and forwards `stderrAgeMs` (derived from `lastStderrAt` recorded by `consolidate-observations.js`) to the inflight consolidation status object, enabling reliable staleness detection distinct from a wall-clock heartbeat timer
- **Progress bar bug**: the N/M chunk ratio marks 100% at the *start* of a chunk rather than the *end*, causing premature completion display — fix is in `consolidation-progress.tsx`'s ratio calculation logic
- **Stage/chunk composite math**: correct progress display requires combining stage index and within-stage chunk ratio rather than using the raw chunk ratio alone

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/consolidation-progress.tsx` — shared progress bar component; chunk ratio calculation
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — forwards `lastStderrAt` and computes `stderrAgeMs` in the inflight status object
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — emits per-stage progress events; heartbeat records `lastStderrAt`; implements per-project root resolution logic to correctly map observations to their respective project roots during consolidation

## Usage
Rendered on the digests and insights dashboard pages whenever a consolidation run is in-flight. The component reads `startedAt`, `ageMs`, `lastMessage`, `stderrAgeMs`, and N/M stage parsing from the heartbeat JSON. After changes to `ObservationConsolidator.js`, restart obs-api to deploy the updated consolidation logic.

## Troubleshooting
- **Progress bar jumps to 100% prematurely**: chunk ratio calculation error in `consolidation-progress.tsx` — ratio should reflect end-of-chunk, not start; verify composite stage/chunk math
- **Progress bar freezes during Stage 2**: Stage 2 `"Insights updated: N"` messages match no regex — extend the parser to recognize these terminal summary lines
- **Staleness detection fires too early**: heartbeat timestamps are wall-clock bumped rather than stderr-activity bumped — verify `lastStderrAt` is being recorded in `consolidate-observations.js` and forwarded by `server.js`
- **Button stuck in "consolidating" after run completes**: heartbeat polling should detect completion; if port 3033 is unreachable the consolidator process may have hung — check for unclosed connections

topic: MCP Constraint Monitor Dashboard — Port 3030 and Service Configuration
summary:
## Purpose
Provides a web UI for the MCP constraint monitor, served on port 3030. The backend API runs separately on port 3031 via `dashboard-server.js`.

## Architecture
- **Port 3030**: Should be served by the Next.js app at `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/`; mapped in `docker-compose` but requires an explicit `supervisord` entry to start it
- **Port 3031**: The API server (`dashboard-server.js`) — this is the only process `supervisord` starts by default; 3030 goes unserved unless the Next.js process is also added
- **Port governance**: All port values must come exclusively from the shared `.env.ports` file; no port values may be hardcoded in `package.json`, `supervisord.conf`, or invented ad-hoc in `.env`

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/` — Next.js app that should serve on port 3030
- `supervisord.conf` — must include a process entry for the Next.js app to activate port 3030
- `Dockerfile.coding-services` — Docker build for the services container
- `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations/.env.ports` — single source of truth for all port assignments
- `temporal.ts`, `routes.ts` — API routing files edited during port debugging

## Usage
- Access the dashboard UI at port 3030; the REST API is at port 3031
- After any `supervisord.conf` change, rebuild/restart the container for changes to take effect
- Port values must be read from `.env.ports` by all consumers

## Troubleshooting
- **Port 3030 returns connection refused**: `supervisord` is not starting the Next.js app — add a `[program:dashboard-ui]` entry pointing to `npm start` (or equivalent) in `supervisord.conf` and rebuild the container
- **Port remapping after docker-compose restart**: Docker may re-map ports unexpectedly; verify the `docker-compose.yml` port mapping and that the internal process is actually listening
- **Unauthorized port entries in `.env`**: Remove any port values not sourced from `.env.ports`; the canonical `.env` must not contain invented `*_PORT` entries
- **UI fix not visible after deployment**: Check for browser caching first — hard refresh with `Cmd+Shift+R` before investigating the bundle

metadata.topic: MCP Constraint Monitor Dashboard — Port 3030 and Service Configuration

topic: VS Code SCM Workspace Configuration — Multi-Repo Visibility
summary:
## Purpose
Ensures the main `coding` repo, `.specstory/history` private repo, and all `integrations/` sub-repos are simultaneously visible in VS Code's Source Control panel without triggering enterprise auth failures or repo-list teardowns.

## Architecture
- **Root cause of disappearing parent repo**: The nested `.specstory/history/.git` repo (pointing at a GHE remote) triggers enterprise auth failures that cause VS Code's git extension to tear down the entire repo list when auto-discovery traverses into `.specstory/`
- **Fix**: Explicitly declare all repos as named `folders` entries in `coding.code-workspace` rather than relying on auto-discovery; disable auto-detection in workspace settings
- **Suppression settings** (retain as safety net): `git.repositoryScanIgnoredFolders: [".specstory"]`, `git.openRepositoryInParentFolders: "never"`, `git.repositoryScanMaxDepth: 0`
- **Secondary bug**: VSCode's submodule auto-detection cascade overrides explicit workspace folder registrations — disabled by workspace setting
- **Final state**: `coding.code-workspace` lists all 8 repos explicitly: `coding`, `.specstory/history`, and 6 `integrations/` sub-repos; SCM panel set to parallel display mode

## Key Files
- `coding.code-workspace` — workspace definition; must list all 8 repos as explicit folder entries with auto-discovery disabled

## Usage
Opened as a workspace (`code coding.code-workspace`) rather than a plain folder. All git operations across all 8 repos are accessible from the SCM panel simultaneously.

## Troubleshooting
- **Parent repo disappears from SCM**: Auto-discovery is traversing into `.specstory/` and hitting GHE auth failures — verify `repositoryScanIgnoredFolders` includes `.specstory` and all repos are listed explicitly in `folders`
- **Sub-repo missing from SCM panel**: Not listed in `coding.code-workspace` `folders` entries — add it explicitly
- **Submodule auto-detection overriding explicit folders**: Disable submodule auto-detection in workspace settings
- **SCM shows duplicate entries**: Both auto-discovery and explicit folder entries are active — confirm auto-discovery is fully disabled

metadata.topic: VS Code SCM Workspace Configuration — Multi-Repo Visibility

topic: Concurrent Agent Session Management — Phantom Commits and Inter-Session Communication
summary:
## Purpose
Documents five hazard patterns that arise when Claude Code agent sessions operate on shared repository state. Each pattern produces distinct failure modes that can silently block phase delivery.

## Architecture

**Pattern 1 — Phantom commit:**
- A runaway parallel session appends commits under the same task ID and git author, often minutes after the active session's final commit
- These commits may re-revert previously authorized changes, producing a loop: commit → revert → report old symptoms — with no net progress across rounds
- Loop confirmation: the same commit hashes appear reverted and re-applied across multiple sequential rounds

**Pattern 2 — Inter-session communication gap:**
- Operator feedback delivered to a prior task instance is not visible to a subsequently spawned session on the same task
- The new session proceeds without the approval or correction and silently repeats prior work
- Mitigation: confirm session identity before delivering feedback; re-deliver to the confirmed active session if a spawn occurred

**Pattern 3 — Worktree drift:**
- Parallel subagents writing to the same worktree produce conflicting file states
- Typically manifests as merge conflicts or non-deterministic test results at wave merge time
- Mitigation: assign each subagent an isolated worktree; enforce isolation at dispatch time

**Pattern 4 — Parallel-wave state-file race:**
- Multiple subagents simultaneously updating shared state files (e.g., `STATE.md`, verification artifacts) cause lost writes via last-write-wins semantics
- Mitigation: serialize all state-file writes to the main worktree at wave boundaries, not within a wave

**Pattern 5 — Competing worker contamination:**
- Orphaned `claude -p` subprocesses from prior agent invocations persist as background workers after their parent session ends
- Unlike phantom commits, these do not modify git state — they produce false or contaminated results in live test assertion checks and live-suite verification tasks
- Non-blocking for git-based tasks (code landing, scaffolding); must be cleared before any live assertion task
- Detection: `pgrep -a claude` lists all running Claude processes with PIDs; cross-reference against known active sessions to identify orphans

## Key Files
- `.specstory/history/` — LSL files for reconstructing which session produced which change
- `.planning/STATE.md` — phase state; inspect for conflicting writes across sessions
- `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md` — checkpoint artifact; verify it reflects the active session's state, not a prior session's

## Usage
Consult when unexpected commit loops, stale feedback, merge conflicts, anomalous test results, or stalled checkpoints appear during wave execution. Each pattern has a distinct signature enabling targeted diagnosis before escalating to a full session restart.

## Troubleshooting
- **Phantom commit loop**: compare `git log` across rounds; if the same hashes cycle, identify and terminate the runaway session before continuing
- **Stale feedback**: re-deliver operator instructions explicitly to the confirmed active session identifier
- **Worktree conflict**: run `git status` in each worktree; reassign overlapping files to separate agents before re-executing the wave
- **State-file race**: restore `STATE.md` from the last known-good checkpoint commit; re-run wave boundary sync
- **Contaminated live assertions**: `pgrep -a claude` → terminate orphaned PIDs → re-run the live assertion task from scratch

metadata.scope: Five hazard patterns arising when Claude Code agent sessions share repository state: phantom commits, inter-session communication gaps, worktree drift, parallel-wave state-file races, and competing worker contamination.

summary:
## Purpose
Wave-based plan execution orchestrator for a locked, planned GSD phase. Loads per-phase `PLAN.md` artifacts, constructs dependency-ordered execution waves, dispatches isolated subagents per wave, and enforces dual-repo commit conventions for phases targeting external implementation repositories.

## Architecture
**Entrypoint**: `gsd-execute-phase` skill; invoked via `/gsd-execute-phase [phase] [flags]`

**Phase context directory**: `.planning/phases/{phase}-*` — all planning artifacts loaded from here at startup.

**CLI flags (strictly token-based — no fuzzy matching, aliasing, or positional fallback):**
- `--wave <N>` — execute a specific wave only; re-entry point after a wave failure
- `--gaps-only` — gap-closure mode; targets only plans marked incomplete without re-executing completed ones
- `--interactive` — user-guided wave-by-wave progression
- No flags → standard full-phase execution (all waves, non-interactive)

**Worktree topology selection (automatic at initialization):**
- **Single-plan topology**: when the phase directory contains exactly one plan file, execution remains in the main worktree; no parallel subagents are spawned
- **Multi-plan topology**: parallel subagents dispatched per plan into isolated worktrees (standard wave behavior)
- **Checkpoint override**: any plan containing a blocking human-verify checkpoint forces main worktree execution regardless of plan count — checkpoint pause/resume is incompatible with subagent dispatch

**Plan task ordering conventions:**
- Non-blocking tasks (code landing, scaffolding) always precede any blocking human-verify checkpoint
- Task 3+ (post-checkpoint) must not begin until `{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md` is updated with operator sign-off
- UAT artifact path: `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md`

**Competing worker contamination:**
- Orphaned `claude -p` subprocesses from prior sessions persist as background workers and can produce false or contaminated results in live assertion checks
- Non-blocking for code landing tasks (Tasks 1–2); must be cleared before any live-suite verification task
- Detection: `pgrep -a claude` — cross-reference PIDs against expected active sessions to identify orphans

## Key Files
- `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-PLAN.md` — wave structure and task definitions (consumed at startup)
- `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md` — human checkpoint state artifact
- `.planning/STATE.md` — phase execution state; updated at each wave boundary

## Usage
Invoked after `gsd-plan-phase` produces a locked `PLAN.md`. Called by the operator or by higher-level orchestrators. Produces committed implementation code, updated `STATE.md`, and phase summary/verification artifacts.

## Troubleshooting
- **No subagents spawned for single-plan phase**: expected behavior — single-plan topology runs in main worktree by design
- **Checkpoint hangs**: confirm `{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md` contains operator sign-off before resuming post-checkpoint tasks
- **Contaminated assertion results**: terminate orphaned `claude -p` processes (`pgrep -a claude`), then re-run the affected live assertion task
- **Wave re-entry after failure**: use `--wave <N>` to re-execute only the failed wave; completed waves are not re-run
- **Dual-repo commit required**: phases targeting an external implementation repo require commits to both the planning repo and the implementation repo before the wave is considered complete

metadata.scope: Wave-based phase execution orchestrator: plan loading, worktree topology selection, subagent dispatch, human-verify checkpoint handling, and dual-repo commit conventions.

- UAT artifact path: `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md`

**Competing worker contamination:**
- Orphaned `claude -p` subprocesses from prior sessions persist as background workers and can produce false or contaminated results in live assertion checks
- Non-blocking for code landing tasks (Tasks 1–2); must be cleared before any live-suite verification task
- Detection: `pgrep -a claude` — cross-reference PIDs against expected active sessions to identify orphans

## Key Files
- `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-PLAN.md` — wave structure and task definitions (consumed at startup)
- `.planning/phases/{phase}-*/{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md` — human checkpoint state artifact
- `.planning/STATE.md` — phase execution state; updated at each wave boundary

## Usage
Invoked after `gsd-plan-phase` produces a locked `PLAN.md`. Called by the operator or by higher-level orchestrators. Produces committed implementation code, updated `STATE.md`, and phase summary/verification artifacts.

## Troubleshooting
- **No subagents spawned for single-plan phase**: expected behavior — single-plan topology runs in main worktree by design
- **Checkpoint hangs**: confirm `{phase}-HUMAN-UAT.md` contains operator sign-off before resuming post-checkpoint tasks
- **Contaminated assertion results**: terminate orphaned `claude -p` processes (`pgrep -a claude`), then re-run the affected live assertion task
- **Wave re-entry after failure**: use `--wave <N>` to re-execute only the failed wave; completed waves are not re-run
- **Dual-repo commit required**: phases targeting an external implementation repo require commits to both the planning repo and the implementation repo before the wave is considered complete

summary:
## Purpose
Verifies insight claims against the observations store. Two operational modes handle the LevelDB exclusive-lock constraint: an in-process HTTP endpoint when the API server is running, and a standalone CLI for offline contexts.

## Architecture
LevelDB permits only one process to hold the store open at a time, driving the dual-mode split:

- **In-process endpoint** (`/verify` on `observations-api-server.mjs`): runs inside the already-running server process which already holds the LevelDB lock — no contention. Invoked via HTTP POST. Includes **project-root resolution**: projects without on-disk checkouts return `null` and are skipped gracefully (not a false negative).
- **Standalone CLI** (`verify-insight-claims.mjs`): opens LevelDB directly; requires exclusive access; incompatible with a concurrently running obs-api.

**Verdict classification:**
- **fresh** — backticked code claims verified against current source
- **partial** — some claims verified, others not matched
- **stale** — claims no longer matching current source
- **gray** — no backticked code claims present; unverifiable by design (**null-ratio rule**); not an error

Both modes execute the same verification logic; mode selection is determined entirely by whether obs-api is currently running.

## Key Files
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — hosts `/verify` endpoint; in-process verification against open LevelDB store; project-root resolution included
- `scripts/verify-insight-claims.mjs` — standalone CLI; offline verification; requires obs-api to be stopped

## Usage
- **Server running**: POST to `/verify` on the obs-api server — no service disruption, no lock conflict.
- **Server stopped**: invoke `verify-insight-claims.mjs` directly.
- Never invoke the standalone CLI while obs-api is running; LevelDB lock conflict crashes the CLI.
- Gray verdicts for code-claims-free insights are correct by design and need no remediation.

## Troubleshooting
- **CLI crashes immediately with LevelDB lock error**: obs-api holds the exclusive store lock — switch to the `/verify` HTTP endpoint
- **`/verify` returns 404**: route absent in the deployed server source — verify it exists in `observations-api-server.mjs`
- **Verification results differ between modes**: both paths read the same LevelDB store; a mismatch indicates the store changed between invocations or the CLI targets a different store path — check `--store` flags
- **All insights for a project render gray**: confirm insights contain backticked code references; if none exist, gray is correct (null-ratio rule) — not a verifier failure
- **Project skipped / returns no results**: project-root resolution returns `null` for projects without on-disk checkouts — expected behavior; project is gracefully excluded

summary:
## Purpose
LSL captures LLM coding session transcripts into per-hour markdown files in `.specstory/history/`, providing session continuity across agent restarts and a human-readable audit trail. It is the event-capture layer of the broader observations pipeline.

## Architecture
- **Session log location**: `.specstory/history/` — markdown files named by time window (e.g., `1100-1200.md`)
- **ETM daemon**: `~/Agentic/coding/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — managed by launchd; writes LSL files as transcript events arrive; health independently verifiable via launchd status and LSL file recency
- **Global LSL Coordinator**: manages ETM lifecycle; registers ETM with PSM; terminates ETM on failed tmux session liveness checks
- **Log flow**: ETM transcript extraction → exchange classification → LSL markdown write to `.specstory/history/`
- **Health indicator**: `[LSL●]` in tmux status bar — driven by `combined-status-line-wrapper.js` → `getLSLHealthStatus()` → `health-prompt-hook.js`

## Key Files
- `~/Agentic/coding/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` — ETM daemon; core LSL writer; managed by launchd
- `.specstory/history/` — active session log storage directory
- `combined-status-line-wrapper.js` — drives `[LSL●]` health indicator in tmux status bar

## Usage
ETM runs as a persistent launchd daemon and writes LSL files automatically during active coding sessions. Files are read at session start (via the `/sl` skill) to re-establish context. The observation pipeline also scans these files during backfill and consolidation cycles.

## Troubleshooting
- **`[LSL●]` shows unhealthy**: check launchd status for the ETM daemon process; verify `.specstory/history/` contains a recently-modified file
- **Most recent LSL files are months old**: if discoverable files are significantly dated (e.g., March when current date is June), either (a) the ETM daemon has been inactive or dropped its launchd registration, or (b) the search is targeting the wrong project directory — confirm `.specstory/history/` is correct for the active project and that ETM is actively writing there; check launchd service status directly
- **Multi-month logging gap recovery**: sessions from the missing period cannot be recovered from ETM output alone; use git history, `PLAN.md`, and `STATE.md` for context reconstruction
- **False `[LSL●]` health reading**: if the indicator is stale, restart the Global LSL Coordinator to force a fresh health write

summary:
## Purpose
`gsd-browser` is a browser automation tool backed by a persistent background Chromium daemon, used to smoke-test web UIs — primarily the Unified Viewer and Coverage dashboard — and produce screenshot evidence for operator verification runbooks at phase acceptance gates. It is the primary mechanism for executing scenario-card checks and generating `{phase}-VERIFICATION.md` artifacts required to formally close a phase.

## Architecture
- **Background Chromium daemon**: a long-running process handling all `navigate` and screenshot operations; liveness is tracked via a PID file in the gsd-browser state directory
- **Daemon lifetime independence**: the daemon can terminate independently of the target service or network; when down, all subsequent `gsd-browser` calls fail with a **process-state error** — not a network or service error
- **Service isolation**: target service HTTP reachability and daemon liveness are fully independent failure domains; a service being up does not imply the daemon is up, and vice versa
- **Fallback path**: if the gsd-browser daemon (also referenced as `gsd-demon` in some planning documents) cannot be fixed, **Playwright** is the identified alternative for web UI smoke-testing and screenshot evidence generation

## Key Files
- PID file in the gsd-browser state directory — primary liveness indicator; check before diagnosing any other failure layer
- `{phase}-VERIFICATION.md` — output artifact produced after successful scenario-card execution; required to formally close a phase

## Usage
Invoked at phase acceptance gates to run scenario-card checks and produce `{phase}-VERIFICATION.md`. Also used for ad-hoc smoke-testing of the Unified Viewer and Coverage dashboard. The daemon must be running before any `navigate` or screenshot command is issued.

## Troubleshooting
- **All calls fail after a period of inactivity**: the daemon likely terminated; restart it rather than diagnosing the target service
- **Process-state error on any operation**: check the PID file in the gsd-browser state directory; do not investigate the target service's HTTP layer until the daemon is confirmed running
- **Daemon cannot be restarted or is permanently broken**: use Playwright as the fallback for scenario-card execution and screenshot capture; it covers the same verification surface

summary:
## Purpose
Provides the Ontology Class filter panel in the Unified Viewer, enabling show/hide of graph nodes by ontological classification in an expandable L0→L1→L2 hierarchy. SC#5 (Phase 60) required ≥15 labels rendered across all hierarchy levels; the gap was a frontend-only tree-building defect with no backend involvement.

## Architecture
**Data layer — correct, no backend changes needed:**
- API emits the complete hierarchy: 1 L0 root, 3 L1 nodes, 10 L2 nodes (Deviation-3 fully resolved)
- Backend response is authoritative; re-probing the API endpoint confirms correct hierarchy before any frontend debugging

**Frontend fix — `l2-subsystem-classifier.ts`:**
- Pre-fix state: 4 flat checkboxes only (`System`, `Component`, `Detail`, `SubComponent`); all L2 children absent; `labelCount` = 4
- Required state: expandable L0→L1→L2 tree; `labelCount` ≥ 15; all L2 nodes present (e.g. `BatchSemanticAnalysis`, `OnlineObservation`)
- `l2-subsystem-classifier.ts` is the **sole artifact** requiring modification; builds the L2 subclass hierarchy consumed by the filter tree renderer
- Fix executed in Plan 60-09 (Wave 5, gated on completed plan 60-07) via wave-based parallel subagent dispatch; the file was confirmed modified during execution

**Integration with graph canvas:**
- Filter selection propagates to `D3GraphCanvas.tsx` via `hiddenNodeTypes` memo
- Layer separation and `hiddenRelationTypes` logic also live in `D3GraphCanvas.tsx` canvas memos

## Key Files
- `l2-subsystem-classifier.ts` — sole fix target; builds the L2 hierarchy for filter tree rendering
- `integrations/unified-viewer/src/D3GraphCanvas.tsx` — `hiddenNodeTypes` / `hiddenRelationTypes` filter memos that consume classifier output; layer separation logic co-located here
- `.planning/phases/60-*/60-09-PLAN.md` — gap-closure plan artifact detailing the SC#5 fix scope

## Usage
Rendered as the ontology class filter panel in the Unified Viewer left sidebar. SC#5 in `60-VERIFICATION.md` is the acceptance gate: `labelCount` ≥ 15 across all hierarchy levels, with expandable tree controls showing L2 children under each L1 node.

## Troubleshooting
- **Flat checkboxes instead of tree hierarchy**: `l2-subsystem-classifier.ts` is not building the L2 level; verify classifier output includes all 10 L2 nodes returned by the API
- **`labelCount` < 15**: expected minimum is L0 (1) + L1 (3) + L2 (≥10) = ≥14 from API data; the classifier must not truncate to top-level labels only; check that L2 children are being enumerated and passed to the renderer
- **API appearing to return incomplete hierarchy**: Deviation-3 is resolved — re-probe the endpoint directly; if 1 L0 + 3 L1 + 10 L2 nodes are absent from the API response, this is a regression, not a known issue
- **SC#5 still PARTIAL after fix**: if `60-VERIFICATION.md` frontmatter has not been updated, `gsd-plan-phase` will hard-stop; see GSD v7.2 Milestone State troubleshooting for the manual repair procedure

summary:
## Purpose
The Coverage page in `system-health-dashboard` visualizes per-project claim verification status using a strict, unambiguous color-to-state mapping. Tiles deep-link to individual insight records using the native km-core UUID `id` as the canonical public identifier. All tile rendering, legend swatches, project-header badge, and deep-link href logic is co-located in a single file.

## Architecture
- **Single rendering file**: all Coverage page visual logic lives in `src/pages/coverage.tsx` within `integrations/system-health-dashboard/`
- **Canonical tile state → color mapping:**
  - `pending` → grey (verifiable; verifier has not yet run — grey is **reserved exclusively** for this state)
  - `unverifiable` / `no claims` → beige/tan (`totalClaims = 0` or non-repo content); explicit high-contrast Tailwind text/background overrides applied; **intentionally distinct from grey** to prevent visual ambiguity with pending
  - `hallucinated` → distinct non-grey color (claims reference code absent from git HEAD history)
  - `verified` → green/positive color (claims confirmed against current repo state)
- **Deep-link**: tile `href` uses the km-core UUID `id` field as the canonical public insight identifier; 117 insights across projects have been deduplicated to use native UUID keys; `legacyId` must not appear in any deep-link path
- **Refresh behavior**: Coverage "Refresh" re-reads cached insight metadata only; does NOT re-execute the verifier pass

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/coverage.tsx` — all tile rendering, legend swatches, project badge, and deep-link href logic

## Usage
Tile states are derived from stored `metadata.codeVerification` fields in km-core records; the Coverage page does not compute them live. A known pending operator check remains: browser-based end-to-end verification of the Coverage tile → deep-link → correct insight record flow has not yet been visually confirmed.

## Troubleshooting
- Tile shows wrong color → check stored `verificationRatio` and `codeVerification` fields in the km-core record; re-run the verifier pass to update them; "Refresh" alone is insufficient
- Deep-link routes to wrong insight or 404s → verify the tile `href` uses the km-core UUID `id` field, not `legacyId`; confirm the insight record exists with that UUID in km-core
- Grey tile appears for a claim that should be `unverifiable` → check if `totalClaims = 0`; if so it should render beige/tan; inspect Tailwind class overrides in `coverage.tsx`
- To verify the deep-link flow end-to-end, use `gsd-browser` to navigate to the Coverage page, click a tile, and confirm the correct insight record loads

topic: Knowledge Graph Semantic Edges — Online Ingest Pipeline
summary:
## Purpose
Observation nodes produced by the online ingest pipeline are persisted with only a single outgoing relation (`capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`). Semantic-content edges (`discusses`, `mentions`, `references`) that should link each Observation to the specific Insights and Components it references are not written. This gap causes all graph traversal from Observation nodes to terminate at `LiveLoggingSystem`, preventing meaningful ancestry resolution and knowledge graph attribution.

## Architecture
- **Affected data scope**: all Observation nodes produced by Pipeline 1 (online ingest); each carries exactly one outgoing relation: `capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`; no semantic-content edges are present on any node
- **Missing edge types**: `discusses`, `mentions`, `references` — semantic-content relations that should link each Observation to relevant Insights and Components
- **Three implicated code locations**:
  - **`persistence-agent.ts`** (lines 928–944) — writes relations per Observation; currently writes only `capturedBy`; the primary target for adding semantic-content edge writes
  - **`coordinator.ts`** — suspected of not classifying Observations against the Insight/Component graph before or during persistence; classification is a prerequisite for resolving correct edge targets
  - **`insight-generation-agent.ts`** — suspected of not creating back-edges from generated Insights to their source Observations; back-edges are required for bidirectional traversal
- **Downstream symptom**: BFS ancestor traversal in the viewer returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for every Observation because no alternate path exists; the traversal logic is correct — the data is deficient
- **Ingest path context**: online ingest flows `ObservationConsolidator.js` → VKB HTTP PUT with `source: 'online'`; the gap is in what relations are written during this path, not in the transport mechanism

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/persistence-agent.ts` — lines 928–944; primary fix target; needs to write `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` in addition to `capturedBy`
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/coordinator.ts` — Observation classification logic; must classify against Insight/Component graph to produce valid edge targets before persistence
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/insight-generation-agent.ts` — Insight generation; needs back-edge writes from each generated Insight to its source Observation(s)
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — Pipeline 1 orchestrator; VKB HTTP PUT entry point
- `general.json` (e.g. `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json`) — raw graph export; use `jq` to confirm absence of `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` edges with Observation sources

## Usage
This is an open defect in the online ingest pipeline. All downstream consumers — viewer graph traversal, VKB queries, timeline-to-entity attribution — receive degraded results until semantic-content edges are added. Recommended fix sequence: (1) audit `coordinator.ts` to understand or implement Observation classification against the Insight/Component graph; (2) pass resolved target IDs into `persistence-agent.ts` for writing semantic-content edges; (3) add back-edge writes in `insight-generation-agent.ts`. Do not modify traversal logic in `ancestry.ts` or viewer components to work around this defect — the fix must be applied upstream.

## Troubleshooting
- **Confirming edge absence**: query `general.json` with `jq` — e.g. `jq '[.edges[] | select(.relation == "discusses" or .relation == "mentions" or .relation == "references")] | length'`; expected result is `0` until the fix is applied
- **Graph traversal always returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for Observation nodes**: confirmed data defect; do not fix by adjusting BFS direction or traversal logic — add the missing edges upstream
- **Identifying correct edge targets**: `coordinator.ts` classification determines which Insight/Component IDs are semantically linked to a given Observation; audit this logic first to ensure targets are well-defined before writing edges in `persistence-agent.ts`
- **Back-edge direction for `insight-generation-agent.ts`**: edges should point Insight → source Observation(s); verify directionality before adding to avoid inverting the semantic relationship
- **Fix interacts with VKB viewer rendering**: after adding semantic-content edges, verify that the VKB graph viewer's filter and legend logic handles the new edge types correctly; `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` are not currently in the viewer's declared legend

metadata.scope: Missing `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` edges on Observation nodes produced by Pipeline 1 (online ingest)
metadata.topic: Knowledge Graph Semantic Edges — Online Ingest Pipeline
metadata.summary:
## Purpose
Observation nodes produced by the online ingest pipeline are persisted with only a single outgoing relation (`capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`). Semantic-content edges (`discusses`, `mentions`, `references`) that should link each Observation to the specific Insights and Components it references are not written. This gap causes all graph traversal from Observation nodes to terminate at `LiveLoggingSystem`, preventing meaningful ancestry resolution and knowledge graph attribution.

## Architecture
- **Affected data scope**: all Observation nodes produced by Pipeline 1 (online ingest); each carries exactly one outgoing relation: `capturedBy → LiveLoggingSystem`; no semantic-content edges are present on any node
- **Missing edge types**: `discusses`, `mentions`, `references` — semantic-content relations that should link each Observation to relevant Insights and Components
- **Three implicated code locations**:
  - **`persistence-agent.ts`** (lines 928–944) — writes relations per Observation; currently writes only `capturedBy`; the primary target for adding semantic-content edge writes
  - **`coordinator.ts`** — suspected of not classifying Observations against the Insight/Component graph before or during persistence; classification is a prerequisite for resolving correct edge targets
  - **`insight-generation-agent.ts`** — suspected of not creating back-edges from generated Insights to their source Observations; back-edges are required for bidirectional traversal
- **Downstream symptom**: BFS ancestor traversal in the viewer returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for every Observation because no alternate path exists; the traversal logic is correct — the data is deficient
- **Ingest path context**: online ingest flows `ObservationConsolidator.js` → VKB HTTP PUT with `source: 'online'`; the gap is in what relations are written during this path, not in the transport mechanism

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/persistence-agent.ts` — lines 928–944; primary fix target; needs to write `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` in addition to `capturedBy`
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/coordinator.ts` — Observation classification logic; must classify against Insight/Component graph to produce valid edge targets before persistence
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/insight-generation-agent.ts` — Insight generation; needs back-edge writes from each generated Insight to its source Observation(s)
- `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — Pipeline 1 orchestrator; VKB HTTP PUT entry point
- `general.json` (e.g. `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json`) — raw graph export; use `jq` to confirm absence of `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` edges with Observation sources

## Usage
This is an open defect in the online ingest pipeline. All downstream consumers — viewer graph traversal, VKB queries, timeline-to-entity attribution — receive degraded results until semantic-content edges are added. Recommended fix sequence: (1) audit `coordinator.ts` to understand or implement Observation classification against the Insight/Component graph; (2) pass resolved target IDs into `persistence-agent.ts` for writing semantic-content edges; (3) add back-edge writes in `insight-generation-agent.ts`. Do not modify traversal logic in `ancestry.ts` or viewer components to work around this defect — the fix must be applied upstream.

## Troubleshooting
- **Confirming edge absence**: query `general.json` with `jq` — e.g. `jq '[.edges[] | select(.relation == "discusses" or .relation == "mentions" or .relation == "references")] | length'`; expected result is `0` until the fix is applied
- **Graph traversal always returns `LiveLoggingSystem` for Observation nodes**: confirmed data defect; do not fix by adjusting BFS direction or traversal logic — add the missing edges upstream
- **Identifying correct edge targets**: `coordinator.ts` classification determines which Insight/Component IDs are semantically linked to a given Observation; audit this logic first to ensure targets are well-defined before writing edges in `persistence-agent.ts`
- **Back-edge direction for `insight-generation-agent.ts`**: edges should point Insight → source Observation(s); verify directionality before adding to avoid inverting the semantic relationship
- **Fix interacts with VKB viewer rendering**: after adding semantic-content edges, verify that the VKB graph viewer's filter and legend logic handles the new edge types correctly; `discusses`/`mentions`/`references` are not currently in the viewer's declared legend

topic: Mention-Candidate System — Filtering Semantics and Data Quality
summary:
## Purpose
Controls which graph entities are admitted into the mention-candidate pool used during wave-analysis mention-classification. Candidates are assembled by `loadMentionCandidates` (filtering on `ontologyClass`) and resolved through `findByOntologyClass`, which applies OR-gate semantics. A widespread `ontologyClass` tagging error contaminates the pool and produces false mention edges.

## Architecture

**Candidate pool assembly (`loadMentionCandidates`):**
- Selects entities using `ontologyClass` as the filter key — not `entityType`
- Current pool: 802 nodes; intended to contain L1/L2/L3 records only
- Actual contamination: 74 Insights tagged `ontologyClass='Detail'` (instead of `'Insight'`), plus 16 Processes, 15 Observations, and 11 Containers with incorrect class tags — all admitted alongside legitimate L1/L2/L3 entries

**`findByOntologyClass` OR-gate semantics:**
- Resolves via `entityType OR ontologyClass` match — not strict class-only filtering
- `isActive` filter: entities without a `validUntil` timestamp unconditionally pass; all 100 current Insight entities lack `validUntil`, so all are always reachable
- Any analysis claiming the bridge reaches fewer than 100 Insights is a misreading of this OR-gate filter

**False-edge impact from contamination:**
- Contaminated pool generates self-referential `Insight→Insight` mention edges and false `Insight→Process` edges
- Total false-edge noise: ~1.2% of all edges — considered acceptable; `ontologyClass` correction deprioritized
- When running coverage analysis, filter to L1/L2/L3 records only to exclude self-referential noise from metrics

**Coverage metric:**
- Tracks Insights with zero mention-candidate coverage ("uncovered Insights")
- Pre-bridge baseline: 37 uncovered Insights; post-bridge execution: 25 uncovered — improvement confirmed from live pipeline changes (bridge execution + writer-path emission activation)

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` — contains `loadMentionCandidates` (filters by `ontologyClass`); calls `findByOntologyClass`; Insight synthesis entry point
- `58-HUMAN-UAT.md` — SC#1 bridge scope verification and coverage improvement tracking

## Usage
The 802-node candidate pool is assembled at wave-analysis runtime. Coverage metrics (uncovered Insight count) verify that bridge execution and writer-path emission activation are taking effect. Filter coverage analysis to L1/L2/L3 records only to avoid inflating uncovered-Insight counts with self-referential noise. The `ontologyClass` data quality fix (retagging 74 Insights from `'Detail'` to `'Insight'`) is tracked but deprioritized.

## Troubleshooting
- **Insights appearing in their own candidate pool**: 74 Insights carry `ontologyClass='Detail'`; they enter the pool and match against themselves; mitigate by filtering coverage analysis to L1/L2/L3 records; permanent fix requires retagging those 74 entities to `ontologyClass='Insight'`
- **False `Insight→Insight` or `Insight→Process` mention edges in graph**: caused by `ontologyClass` mistagging; current impact ~1.2% of total edges; deprioritized pending further real-world impact assessment
- **Bridge appears to reach fewer than 100 Insights**: verify OR-gate semantics in `findByOntologyClass` (`entityType OR ontologyClass`); confirm all Insights lack `validUntil` (so all pass `isActive`); all 100 Insights are reachable — any lower count is a filter or analysis error
- **Coverage metrics not improving after bridge execution**: confirm writer-path emission was activated before the bridge run; expected post-bridge baseline is ~25 uncovered (down from 37)
- **`loadMentionCandidates` admitting unexpected entity types**: the filter operates on `ontologyClass`, not `entityType`; entities with wrong `ontologyClass` values are admitted regardless of their structural type

summary:
## Purpose
Prevents OpenCode sessions from crashing unrecoverably when the Copilot API returns HTTP 400 on oversized compaction requests, by pre-truncating message payloads before the compaction call fires.

## Architecture
- **Root cause**: When message context grows large, OpenCode triggers compaction; the compaction request itself exceeds the Copilot API's payload limit, returning HTTP 400; OpenCode's `isOverflow()` only matches `OVERFLOW_PATTERNS` or `statusCode === 413`, so the 400 is classified as a non-retryable `api_error`
- **Fix**: Plugin hooks `experimental.chat.messages.transform` to truncate oversized individual message parts using 3-pass progressive truncation logic; per-part maximum is 30K characters; total payload budgets are 800K (normal) / 400K (proxy-safe)
- **Registration**: Plugin path must be an absolute `file://` URI in the `"plugin"` array of `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`
- **Deployment**: `install.sh` copies `plugins/opencode/compaction-guard.js` to `~/.opencode/plugins/` and patches `opencode.json`; `uninstall.sh` reverses both steps
- **Database hygiene**: The conversation database at `~/.opencode/data/conversation-default.db` can grow to 1GB+ with an active WAL file; periodic `VACUUM` and log/session-diff purges are needed to prevent OOM and bloat

## Key Files
- `plugins/opencode/compaction-guard.js` — plugin source in the coding repo
- `~/.opencode/plugins/compaction-guard.js` — deployed location read by OpenCode
- `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` — must contain `"plugin": ["file:///...path.../compaction-guard.js"]`
- `~/.opencode/data/conversation-default.db` — SQLite conversation store; monitor size and WAL
- `coding/install.sh` / `coding/uninstall.sh` — deploy/remove the plugin globally

## Usage
Installed globally via `install.sh`. Active for all OpenCode sessions on the machine. The `reserved` parameter in OpenCode config controls how early compaction triggers but does not reduce payload size — only this plugin pre-truncates the payload.

## Troubleshooting
- **"Bad Request" during compaction persists**: Verify the plugin is listed in `opencode.json` with an absolute `file://` path; confirm the file exists at `~/.opencode/plugins/`
- **Plugin not loading**: OpenCode requires the `file://` URI scheme — relative paths are not resolved
- **OOM or compaction crash despite plugin**: Check `~/.opencode/data/conversation-default.db` size; if over ~500MB, run `VACUUM` via sqlite3 and purge `~/.opencode/logs/` and session diffs

summary:
## Purpose
The observations pipeline converts raw session events into structured observation records persisted in the observations database. It is distinct from LSL file writing: the ETM can be healthy and writing LSL files while this downstream pipeline is stalled.

## Architecture
- **Layer 1 — ETM**: event-capture daemon managed by launchd; writes LSL files to `.specstory/history/` and emits events downstream; health confirmed independently via launchd status and LSL file recency
- **Layer 2 — obs-api**: intermediate HTTP service layer; receives events from ETM and writes records to the observations database; has inspectable service log tails
- **Layer 3 — observations database**: terminal storage layer; persists observation records for downstream consumers
- **Failure independence**: each layer fails independently; a multi-hour gap in observation records does not imply ETM failure — ETM health must be ruled out first before investigating obs-api or the database
- **Launchd**: manages ETM daemon lifecycle; daemon status is the first check when diagnosing pipeline stalls

## Key Files
- `.specstory/history/` — LSL file output directory; examine most recent file write timestamps to verify Layer 1 health
- obs-api service logs — tail to identify Layer 2 failure point (exact log path resolved at runtime from launchd config)
- Observations database — Layer 3 storage; last-write timestamp is the key diagnostic indicator

## Usage
Runs continuously in the background. Downstream consumers of the observations database include session continuity bootstrap, knowledge context injection (embedding pipeline), and digest/insight generation. A stalled pipeline causes all downstream consumers to operate on stale data.

## Troubleshooting
- **No new observations despite healthy ETM**: stall is downstream of ETM — inspect obs-api service logs first, then check observations database for last-write timestamp and connectivity
- **Confirming ETM health**: check launchd daemon status and verify LSL files in `.specstory/history/` have recent write timestamps; both must be true to rule out Layer 1
- **Isolating the failure layer**: tail ETM logs then obs-api logs in sequence; the last layer showing activity at the gap boundary is healthy — the next layer is the stall point
- **Confirming gap duration**: query the observations database for its most recent record timestamp; a gap of several hours confirms a true stall rather than normal latency
- **Recovering after obs-api stall**: restart obs-api service via launchd (or equivalent); verify new observation records appear in the database after restart before closing the investigation

metadata.scope: Three-layer pipeline (ETM → obs-api → observations database) that converts raw session events into persisted structured observation records.

- **Confirming gap duration**: query the observations database for its most recent record timestamp; a gap of several hours confirms a true stall rather than normal latency
- **Recovering after obs-api stall**: restart obs-api service via launchd (or equivalent); verify new observation records appear in the database after restart before closing the investigation

topic: Observation and Digest Retention — SQLite Pruning and JSON Cold-Store
summary:
## Purpose
Migrates the legacy SQLite observation store (observations, digests, insights) into the km-core graph store (LevelDB via `GraphKMStore`). Provides a safe dry-run mode that validates projected entity counts against a backup DB without requiring obs-api to be stopped or the live store to be touched.

## Architecture
- **504-line Node.js script** at `scripts/migrate-sqlite-to-kmcore.mjs`
- **`--dry-run` mode**: operates source-only — reads from the backup DB, prints projected counts, skips all `GraphKMStore`/LevelDB initialization; obs-api can remain running during a dry-run
- **`--dry-run` guard (critical)**: `GraphKMStore` construction and `store.open()` are deferred behind a conditional; without this guard, `--dry-run` initializes LevelDB and fails when obs-api holds the lock
- **Live mode**: constructs `GraphKMStore`, calls `store.open()`, and requires obs-api to be stopped before execution
- **Idempotency**: enforced via `legacyId` matching (`{system:'A', id}` per Phase 41 D-13)
- **Provenance stamping**: `{provider:'migration', model:'sqlite-to-kmcore', runId, timestamp}`
- **CLI flags**: `--dry-run`, `--db`, `--store`, `--resume`; logs progress every 100 rows to stderr; exits non-zero on errors
- **Filename constraint note**: the substring `lite-` in `sqlite-to` triggers a `no-parallel-files` pattern; during authoring, the file was written under the interim name `migrate-legacy-to-kmcore.mjs` then renamed via shell `mv`

**Validated projected entity counts** (dry-run against backup DB):
- observations: 913, digests: 272, insights: 78 → **1,263 total**

**Task completion status:**
- **Tasks 1 & 2** — script committed and dry-run smoke test PASSED (1,263 projected, zero errors); backup of `.observations/observations.db` created
- **Tasks 3 and 4** — DEFERRED; require operator-coordinated service restarts before live migration; legacy `DROP` commands with verification gates deferred to Plan 44-11

## Key Files
- `scripts/migrate-sqlite-to-kmcore.mjs` — migration script; `--dry-run` guard defers `GraphKMStore` init behind conditional
- `.observations/observations.db` — live SQLite source (do not target directly during dry-run)
- `.data/backups/observations.db.pre-phase44.[timestamp]` — timestamped binary backup; not committed; covered by `.gitignore`
- `.planning/phases/44-rest-api-git-snapshots/44-10-SUMMARY.md` — Task 2 checkpoint; lists deferred work for Tasks 3 and 4; documents backup path, size, and sha256

## Usage
Run `--dry-run` first against the backup DB to validate projected counts and confirm zero errors before any live migration. Live migration (Tasks 3 and 4) requires obs-api to be stopped. After live migration, verify entity counts in the km-core store match the 1,263 dry-run projection. The script integrates with `GraphKMStore` from `@fwornle/km-core` (local workspace at `lib/km-core/`). Use `--resume` for idempotent incremental backfill after the initial migration.

## Troubleshooting
- **`--dry-run` initializes LevelDB / fails with lock error**: `GraphKMStore` construction not gated behind the dry-run conditional — verify the initialization guard is present in the script
- **Projected count mismatch between dry-run runs**: Confirm the backup DB is the target for dry-run, not the live DB (which may be updated by obs-api between runs)
- **`store.open()` fails during live migration**: obs-api process is still holding the LevelDB lock — stop obs-api before running live mode
- **`@fwornle/km-core` import fails**: `node_modules/@fwornle/km-core` symlink absent — create pointing to `lib/km-core/`
- **Script cannot be written due to `no-parallel-files` filename constraint**: Write under interim name `migrate-legacy-to-kmcore.mjs`, then rename via shell `mv`; the constraint triggers on `lite-` substring in `sqlite-to`
- **Tasks 3 and 4 scope unclear**: Consult `44-10-SUMMARY.md` for the deferred work scope and the Rule-4 checkpoint before starting live migration

summary:
## Purpose
Provides semantic vector search over the project's accumulated observations, digests, insights, and KG entities to enable context injection into agent prompts. Backs the standalone retrieval HTTP service (port 3848) consumed by all agent adapter layers.

## Architecture
- **Embedding service** (`src/embedding-service.ts`): uses `fastembed` for local, platform-native text embedding
- **fastembed cache**: pre-warmed during the Docker **builder stage** and baked into the production image (~90MB); the `local_cache` directory is **not** bind-mounted at runtime — cache must exist in the image or the process fails with `FATAL` at startup
- **Qdrant**: stores vectors in 4 collections: `observations`, `digests`, `insights`, `kg_entities`
- **SHA-256 content hashing** (`src/content-hash.ts`): provides idempotency — already-embedded items are skipped on re-run
- **Write-time hook**: a Redis pub/sub listener (`embedding-listener` supervisord process) auto-embeds new observations as they are written
- **Proxy env vars**: `supervisord.conf` inherits proxy environment variables (with `NO_PROXY` covering internal services) rather than clearing them

## Key Files
- `src/embedding-service.ts` — core embedding logic, fastembed integration
- `src/content-hash.ts` — SHA-256 idempotency hashing
- `src/qdrant-collections.ts` — Qdrant collection schema definitions
- `embedding-config.json` — embedding model and collection configuration (path must be relative to compiled output location)
- `docker/Dockerfile.coding-services` — builder stage pre-warms fastembed cache; copies to production image
- `docker/supervisord.conf` — starts `embedding-listener`; inherits proxy env vars

## Usage
The backfill CLI is run once (or after bulk data imports). The `embedding-listener` Redis pub/sub daemon runs as a background supervisord process, embedding new observations automatically. The retrieval service on port 3848 is called by agent hook adapters.

## Troubleshooting
- **`embedding-listener` shows `FATAL` / "Exited too <USER_ID_REDACTED>"**: fastembed local cache is missing from the image — rebuild `Dockerfile.coding-services` so the builder stage pre-warms and copies the cache (~90MB)
- **External fetch fails from inside container**: verify `supervisord.conf` inherits `http_proxy`/`https_proxy` and sets `NO_PROXY` for internal hosts
- **`embedding-config.json` not found at runtime**: `__dirname` in compiled output resolves to `dist/embedding/` — use `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../embedding-config.json')` or an env var absolute path
- **New observations not appearing in Qdrant**: verify `embedding-listener` is `RUNNING` via supervisorctl; check Redis pub/sub publication; manually trigger backfill CLI for missed items
- **fastembed fails to load tokenizer binaries**: `--ignore-scripts` npm install flag blocks postinstall scripts — use a separate install step without that flag for fastembed

topic: km-core Knowledge Graph Hydration — JSON Export Priority and Build Safety
summary:
## Purpose
The `hydrate()` function in `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` controls whether the in-memory knowledge graph loads from LevelDB cache or the canonical JSON export. Without a patch, `hydrate()` defaults to LevelDB, which may be stale — causing catastrophic graph collapse. The patch is build-fragile: `npm run build` regenerates the compiled dist artifact and discards any manual patch to that file, requiring both the TypeScript source and the compiled artifact to be patched independently after every build.

## Architecture
- **Unpatched behavior**: prefers LevelDB cache; stale cache produces collapse (observed: ~1270 nodes/1594 edges → 924 nodes/3 edges)
- **Patched behavior**: prefers `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json` as the authoritative load source
- **Build fragility**: `npm run build` compiles `persistence.ts` → `dist/store/persistence.js`; any manual patch to the dist file is overwritten; the source patch survives the build but the dist patch does not
- **Two required patch targets simultaneously**:
  - `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` — TypeScript source
  - `lib/km-core/dist/store/persistence.js` — compiled artifact; must be re-patched after every `npm run build`
- **Recovery sequence**: (1) re-apply patch to both files, (2) wipe stale LevelDB, (3) rehydrate from `general.json`, (4) restart `obs-api` and VKB
- **Healthy post-recovery graph state**: 816 nodes / 1262 edges; edge type distribution: `contains: 849`, `related_to: 202`, `capturedBy: 112`, `has_insight: 67`, `parent-child: 11`
- **Open writer-side issue**: `ObservationConsolidator`/`ObservationWriter` still actively emit `Observation` and `Digest` entities to km-core post-architectural-removal; filter these from the JSON export before loading until the writer-side fix lands

## Key Files
- `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` — `hydrate()` source; patch to prefer JSON over LevelDB
- `lib/km-core/dist/store/persistence.js` — compiled artifact; must also be patched after every `npm run build`
- `.data/exports/knowledge-export/general.json` — canonical JSON export; authoritative rehydration source

## Usage
After every `npm run build` in the km-core workspace, verify that `dist/store/persistence.js` still contains the JSON-preference patch before restarting `obs-api` or VKB. The patch has not been absorbed into the build process — manual post-build verification and re-patching of the dist file is required on every build. All Insights should be tagged `source='auto'` after a clean rehydration to clear any false "Manual" badges.

## Troubleshooting
- **Graph collapses after a build (nodes/edges drop dramatically)**: `npm run build` overwrote the dist patch; re-apply to `lib/km-core/dist/store/persistence.js`, wipe LevelDB, rehydrate from `general.json`, restart `obs-api` and VKB
- **Source patched but graph still collapses**: patching `persistence.ts` alone is insufficient if the dist was compiled before the patch — always patch `dist/store/persistence.js` directly regardless of source state
- **After rehydration, spurious `Observation`/`Digest` entities appear**: `ObservationConsolidator`/`ObservationWriter` are actively emitting these (writer-side root cause unresolved); filter them from the JSON export before loading
- **`obs-api` or VKB still reflecting stale/collapsed graph after rehydration**: both services hold in-memory state; restart both after rehydration to pick up the recovered graph
- **`isProject` static ESM import failure in `mcp-servers:semantic-analysis` container**: open unresolved issue introduced during Phase 57; container fails on `isProject` import; no confirmed workaround

metadata.scope: The `hydrate()` function in `lib/km-core/src/store/persistence.ts` and its build-fragile patch state.

topic: Git Security — OAuth Token Scrubbing and Redaction Patterns
summary:
## Purpose
Prevents OAuth tokens and personal identifiers from persisting in git history or the observations database, and configures ongoing redaction for future writes.

## Architecture
Two distinct layers:
1. **Git history scrubbing**: Uses `git-filter-repo` with a replacements file to rewrite all commits and remove token strings matching `gho_`, `ghp_`, `github_pat_` prefixes. After rewriting, force-push to remote.
2. **Runtime redaction**: `ConfigurableRedactor` applies patterns from `redaction-patterns.json` to observation content before DB write. Patterns cover user IDs, email addresses, file paths, and OAuth token prefixes.

Retroactive DB cleanup (for data written before the redactor was active) requires a direct SQL update against the `messages` column.

## Key Files
- `redaction-patterns.json` — pattern list for `ConfigurableRedactor`; must include `gho_`, `ghp_`, `github_pat_` for OAuth tokens
- `~/Agentic/coding/.specstory/config/redaction-patterns.json` — specstory-specific redaction config
- `ObservationWriter.js` — applies `ConfigurableRedactor` on new observation writes
- `.env` — may contain redaction-related environment variables

## Usage
`ConfigurableRedactor` runs automatically on every new observation write. For historical cleanup, run targeted SQL updates or a script against the observations SQLite DB. For git history cleanup, run `git-filter-repo --replace-text <replacements-file>` then force-push.

## Troubleshooting
- **Raw user IDs or emails appear in stored observations**: Data was written before the redactor was configured; run retroactive SQL cleanup on the `messages` column
- **OAuth token still found in git history after filter-repo**: Verify the replacements file covers the exact token string; re-run `git log -S <token>` to confirm removal before force-pushing
- **Second force-push appears to fail after filter-repo**: The first push likely succeeded; verify remote state with `git fetch` before retrying

topic: Knowledge Graph API and Working Memory Service
summary:
## Purpose
Exposes the project's knowledge graph (Memgraph) entities via a REST API so that the working memory module and other consumers can retrieve structured project context (Projects, Components, SubComponents, etc.) for injection into agent context windows.

## Architecture
- **Entity API**: `/api/entities` route in `api-routes.js`; accepts `types` (plural, CSV) parameter — e.g., `?types=Component,SubComponent`; also accepts legacy `type` (singular) for backward compatibility
- **KG contents**: 673 correctly-typed entities — 1 Project (Coding), 8 Component, 353 SubComponent, 310 Detail, 1 System
- **GraphDatabaseService deduplication**: Project nodes were previously omitted from `?types=Project` results due to second-pass deduplication logic in `GraphDatabaseService.js`; fix requires Docker rebuild to deploy
- **Client-side leak prevention**: `working-memory.js` filters out Project/System nodes that leak into component-type result lists
- **Docker deployment**: `coding-services` container is NOT bind-mounted; changes to `GraphDatabaseService.js` require a full Docker rebuild and restart

## Key Files
- `api-routes.js` — `/api/entities` route; accepts both `type` and `types` parameters
- `GraphDatabaseService.js` — executes Cypher <USER_ID_REDACTED>; deduplication logic must not drop Project nodes
- `src/retrieval/working-memory.js` — calls `/api/entities?types=Component` and filters results client-side to exclude Project and System nodes

## Usage
Called by `working-memory.js` at injection time to build the 300-token KG prefix. Also available for direct querying during debugging or cleanup scripts. The `types=` (plural) parameter is the canonical form; `type=` (singular) is supported for backward compatibility only.

## Troubleshooting
- **`working-memory.js` returns 0 components**: Verify the API call uses `types=` (plural); `type=` singular was the original bug
- **Project entity missing from `?types=Project` results**: Second-pass dedup in `GraphDatabaseService.js` was stripping Project nodes — verify the fix is deployed (requires Docker rebuild)
- **System entity appearing in component lists**: Client-side filter in `working-memory.js` must explicitly exclude `Project` and `System` node types
- **Changes to `GraphDatabaseService.js` not reflected**: Container is not bind-mounted; run `docker-compose build && docker-compose up -d` to redeploy
